title: Honors: who plays first, and when it doesn't matter
microFact: Lowest score on the previous hole tees off first — that privilege is called 'the honor'.
lesson:
Traditional order of play:

• FIRST TEE: order is agreed casually — flip a tee, or just "you're up."
• EVERY TEE AFTER: the player with the best score on the previous hole has THE HONOR and tees first. Ties = previous order stands.
• DURING THE HOLE: the player FARTHEST from the hole plays first ("you're away").

Modern overlay: READY GOLF (byte 8) relaxes all of this in casual stroke play — if you're ready and it's safe, hit. But you should still KNOW the traditional order, for two reasons. First, in MATCH PLAY, order is a real rule — your opponent can make you replay a shot played out of turn. Second, honors culture survives on the tee box at traditional clubs: if a member earns the honor, let them take it. Saying "your honor" with a nod after they birdie is exactly the fluency you're building.

When in doubt, a cheerful "ready golf?" on the first tee sets expectations for the whole round.

quiz.question: In traditional order of play, who hits first during a hole (after tee shots)?
options: Whoever had the honor on the tee
The player farthest from the hole
The best player in the group
The player whose ball is safest
quiz.explanation: Farthest from the hole plays first — 'you're away.' The honor only decides tee-shot order.
